script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'phase2b-safe-renames',
  description: 'Execute the low-risk Plan V4 renames (SAFE-static symbols + TEXT-ONLY), gated by test-collection + oracle SHA + codex',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Setup', detail: 'phase2 worktree + parse SAFE-static/TEXT-ONLY rows + overlay oracle' },
    { title: 'Rename', detail: 'execute renames with per-rename verify + revert-on-failure' },
    { title: 'Validate', detail: 'test collection unchanged + oracle replay SHA==golden' },
    { title: 'Codex-Gate', detail: 'codex confirms renames are reference-complete + score-safe' },
    { title: 'Record', detail: 'commit + open PR' },
  ],
}

const PY = '/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python'
const GOLDEN = '9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98'
const WORKLIST = '/home/polaris/polaris_project/NAME_RENAME_WORKLIST_validated.tsv'
const WT = '/home/polaris/wt/phase2'

phase('Setup')
const setup = await agent(
  `Set up a worktree to execute low-risk code renames for a code-review-readiness cleanup.
1. cd /workspace/POLARIS && git worktree remove ${WT} --force 2>/dev/null; git branch -D chore/review-readiness-phase2 2>/dev/null; git worktree add ${WT} -b chore/review-readiness-phase2 chore/review-readiness-phase1   (builds on the migrated Phase 1 code).
2. Overlay the pinned oracle for validation: cd ${WT} && git checkout chore/review-readiness-phase0 -- tests/oracle/ ; cp -n /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_llm.jsonl /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_retrieval.jsonl /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_golden.json ${WT}/tests/oracle/cassettes/ 2>/dev/null ; ls ${WT}/tests/oracle/cassettes/.
3. Parse ${WORKLIST}: identify the rows whose risk class is SAFE (pure code symbol renames) and TEXT-ONLY (docstring/log-tag jargon). For each, extract old_name, new_name, and location/file. Return the two lists (SAFE-static and TEXT-ONLY) as structured rows. Report the exact counts (expected ~105 SAFE + 12 TEXT-ONLY per the plan; report the actual numbers you find).
4. Establish the BASELINE: 'cd ${WT} && timeout 200 ${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q 2>&1 | tail -1' — record the collected count + error count (this is the green baseline renames must not regress).
Return the parsed rows + baseline collection numbers.`,
  { label: 'setup', phase: 'Setup', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['safe_rows','text_rows','safe_count','text_count','baseline_collection'], properties:{ safe_rows:{type:'array',items:{type:'object',additionalProperties:true}}, text_rows:{type:'array',items:{type:'object',additionalProperties:true}}, safe_count:{type:'integer'}, text_count:{type:'integer'}, baseline_collection:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Rename')
const rename = await agent(
  `Execute low-risk renames in ${WT} (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2). You have these rows to rename (from the worklist):
TEXT-ONLY (docstring/log-tag edits only, zero runtime risk): ${JSON.stringify(setup.text_rows)}
SAFE-static (pure code symbols with static references only): ${JSON.stringify(setup.safe_rows)}
Baseline collection to preserve: ${setup.baseline_collection}
Method (mirror the proven config-migration discipline):
1. TEXT-ONLY: replace the old jargon token with the new one ONLY in docstrings/comments/log strings at the cited location. Do not touch identifiers.
2. SAFE-static symbols: for each old_name->new_name, rename the DEFINITION and ALL references within src/ (and scripts/tests if they reference it). Use word-boundary-aware replacement (\\b) so you never partial-match. NEVER rename inside string literals unless it is a TEXT-ONLY row. If a symbol's grep shows it is referenced as a STRING anywhere (dynamic), SKIP it and record it as skipped (it should have been NEEDS-ALIAS, not SAFE).
3. After EACH batch of ~10 renames: run 'cd ${WT} && timeout 200 ${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q 2>&1 | tail -1'. If the error count rose above baseline, find the offending rename, REVERT just it (git diff to locate), and record it as skipped. Keep collection at baseline.
4. py_compile any changed file; revert any that fail to compile.
Be conservative: when unsure a rename is reference-complete, SKIP and record it rather than risk breakage. Return the counts applied vs skipped, and the list of skipped names with reasons.`,
  { label: 'rename', phase: 'Rename', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['text_applied','safe_applied','skipped','collection_after'], properties:{ text_applied:{type:'integer'}, safe_applied:{type:'integer'}, skipped:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, collection_after:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Validate')
const validate = await agent(
  `Validate the renames in ${WT} are score-safe.
1. Full test COLLECTION unchanged: 'cd ${WT} && timeout 200 ${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q 2>&1 | tail -1' — must match the baseline error count (${setup.baseline_collection}). Report it.
2. Config characterization still green: 'cd ${WT} && timeout 120 ${PY} -m pytest tests/test_config_registry.py tests/test_settings_models.py -q 2>&1 | tail -1'.
3. ORACLE replay on the covered path: 'cd ${WT} && PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=3 ${PY} tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py --replay 2>&1 | tail -20'. Confirm the artifact SHA still equals the golden ${GOLDEN} and both controls pass (this proves the renames didn't move behavior on the covered outline path). If the outline agent's own symbols were renamed and the oracle files reference old names, note that (the oracle path may need the same rename to stay consistent).
Return whether all three gates pass + the collection numbers + oracle SHA.`,
  { label: 'validate', phase: 'Validate', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['collection_ok','characterization_ok','oracle_sha','oracle_matches','notes'], properties:{ collection_ok:{type:'boolean'}, characterization_ok:{type:'boolean'}, oracle_sha:{type:'string'}, oracle_matches:{type:'boolean'}, notes:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Codex-Gate')
const gate = await agent(
  `Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate a batch of low-risk code renames. Write /tmp/rename_gate.md then run 'cd /tmp && timeout 240 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/rename_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -30' (bubblewrap warning fine; embed evidence inline; medium effort; no sandbox flags).
Context: We executed TEXT-ONLY + SAFE-static renames from a codex-pre-validated worklist. Rename result: ${JSON.stringify(rename)}. Validation: ${JSON.stringify(validate)}. The renames were required to keep pytest --collect-only at baseline error count and to keep the deterministic oracle replay byte-identical (golden ${GOLDEN}) on the covered path.
Ask codex: "Given test-collection is unchanged from baseline, config characterization passes, and the oracle replay is still byte-identical, are these SAFE-static + TEXT-ONLY renames reference-complete and score-safe to commit? Any residual risk (a symbol renamed but a dynamic/string reference missed)? End with: RENAMES-SAFE or RENAMES-REVISE."
Return codex's verdict + points.`,
  { label: 'codex-gate', phase: 'Codex-Gate', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['verdict','codex_points'], properties:{ verdict:{type:'string'}, codex_points:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}} } } })

phase('Record')
const rec = await agent(
  `Record the Phase 2B renames. Worktree ${WT} (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2).
Only proceed to commit if validation gates passed (collection_ok=${validate.collection_ok}, oracle_matches=${validate.oracle_matches}) and codex verdict was RENAMES-SAFE (${gate.verdict}). If not, commit nothing and report the blocker.
1. Remove the overlaid oracle tooling from staging if it wasn't already committed (we don't want to duplicate tests/oracle on this branch): check 'git status'; only stage src/ + scripts/ + tests/ rename changes, NOT the overlaid tests/oracle/* unless renames actually touched them. Grep-check no secret.
2. Write docs/review_readiness/phase2_renames_applied.md summarizing: which classes, counts applied/skipped, the gates that passed (collection, characterization, oracle SHA), codex verdict.
3. Commit ("Phase 2B: SAFE-static + TEXT-ONLY renames (score-safe, oracle-validated)") with trailers:
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01TLpJSqNfJVSP1UGdtk89nA
4. Push -u origin chore/review-readiness-phase2 and open a PR to main via '/home/polaris/.local/bin/gh pr create --repo aldrinor/deep-cove-research --base main --head chore/review-readiness-phase2 --title "Code-review readiness: Phase 2B low-risk renames" --body "SAFE-static + TEXT-ONLY renames from the codex-validated worklist; test-collection unchanged, config characterization green, oracle replay byte-identical. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)"'.
Return {commit_sha, pushed, pr_url, committed_or_blocked}.`,
  { label: 'record', phase: 'Record', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['commit_sha','pushed','pr_url','committed_or_blocked'], properties:{ commit_sha:{type:'string'}, pushed:{type:'boolean'}, pr_url:{type:'string'}, committed_or_blocked:{type:'string'} } } })

return { setup: { safe_count: setup.safe_count, text_count: setup.text_count, baseline: setup.baseline_collection }, rename, validate, gate, record: rec }

setup.baseline: Worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2 (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2, built on chore/review-readiness-phase1). Baseline command: cd /home/polaris/wt/phase2 && timeout 200 /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q | tail-1. RESULT: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 31.95s. This is the green baseline floor (collected=16738, errors=11) that renames must not regress. The 11 collection errors are pre-existing environment/dependency failures NOT caused by the rename work: tests/integration/test_mesh_e2e.py, tests/polaris_graph/test_demo_smoke.py, tests/polaris_v6/api/test_auth.py, tests/scripts/test_run_paid_evaluator_scoring.py, tests/test_clean_state.py (playwright TargetClosedError), tests/unit/test_clarification_agent.py, tests/unit/test_hle_benchmark.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_api.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_cli.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_snapshot.py, tests/unit/test_perspective_tracking.py.

skipped: _run_honest_sweep_r3 (iwire014_quantified_replay.py:63) — TEXT-ONLY row but the token does not exist in the file; only references to the MODULE filename run_honest_sweep_r3.py exist (lines 31/53/59). No valid rename target; renaming the module file is out of scope.

build_and_run_v4 -> build_and_run (live_server.py:557) AND build_and_run_v4 -> build_and_run_pipeline_a_ui (pipeline_a_ui_adapter.py:187) — SKIPPED as one linked cluster. It is a LIVE import (not a docstring) and the symbol is asserted by a source-grep STRING in tests/polaris_graph/test_b102_graph_v4.py:197 ('pipeline_a_ui_adapter import build_and_run_v4' in source). Dynamic/source-inspection reference => NEEDS-ALIAS, not safe.

POLARIS BEAT-BOTH -> POLARIS Benchmark Report (report_renderer.py:126) — labeled SAFE-static const but the token lives inside an HTML <title> STRING LITERAL, not a code symbol. Rule forbids editing string literals for SAFE-static rows; belongs in TEXT-ONLY class.

HonestSweepJobRunner / HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig / make_default_honest_sweep_job_runner (audit_ir/__init__.py:29-32 and honest_sweep_job_runner.py:177/450) — SKIPPED as a cluster. Worklist gives CONFLICTING targets: __init__ re-export maps to SweepJobRunner but the definition maps to V30SweepJobRunner (and make_default to make_default_sweep_job_runner vs make_default_v30_sweep_job_runner). A re-export must match its definition; the two rows disagree. 15 references across a public API — needs human reconciliation of the single canonical name before applying.

_screen_junk_evidence (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:1248 and :14016) — externally referenced by a source-grep STRING harness: scripts/iarch_beatboth011_junk_source_screen_harness.py lines 468-470 assert 'def _screen_junk_evidence(' in src and src.count('_screen_junk_evidence('). Renaming breaks the harness's textual assertions. NEEDS-ALIAS.

_detect_ci_junk (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:14655) — the cited line is a comment block, not a definition. The actual _detect_ci_junk is only a LOCAL import ALIAS (line 15655: 'import detect_content_integrity_junk as _detect_ci_junk'); the real symbol is detect_content_integrity_junk defined in src/polaris_graph/retrieval/. Location/target mismatch and the real rename is a larger cross-module change — needs human review.

_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21675) — referenced as a STRING for dynamic namespace lookup in tests/polaris_graph/test_wave_b_ws2_paid_slate.py (ns['_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS'], and listed in a _WANTED string set). Dynamic reference => NEEDS-ALIAS.

_ARTIFACT_KIND_HEADINGS (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:6700) — no-op row (old_name == new_name) with a malformed SUGGESTED field ('Declined — no report produced'). No actionable rename; needs human review (as the worklist note flagged).

OpenAIShimClient (scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_compose_openai_validation.py:58) — VERDICT=RENAME but SUGGESTED target is '-' (no target). In a retired directory covered by a separate dir-retirement issue. No rename target.

dice -> StageCheckResult (pipeline_diced_preflight.py:217) — no class literally named 'dice' at that location; the nearby class is DiceResult and line 217 is a comment ('Dice result + context'). 'dice' is also used pervasively as the English domain word across src/ comments/docstrings. Ambiguous target; a real rename would be DiceResult->StageCheckResult (bigger than specified). Needs human review.

contracts_v3 -> contracts (analysis_notebook.py:14) — 'contracts_v3' here is a MODULE name ('from src.polaris_graph.contracts_v3 import AnalysisEntry'), referenced across ~20 files (nodes/, tests/v3/). Target 'contracts' is generic (worklist itself hedged: 'or descriptive submodule name') and would require a module-file rename with collision risk. Too broad/ambiguous to apply safely.

is_junk -> is_chrome_screen (weighted_enrichment.py:5212) — 'is_junk' is reused as a local var / function parameter across MULTIPLE overlapping scopes in the same file (lines 4860 param, 4911, 5213, 5245, 5261, 5349, 5379, 5396) and also appears as an OBJECT ATTRIBUTE (getattr(verdict,'is_junk',...)) in run_honest_sweep_r3.py. Cannot line-scope a single-var rename safely with word-boundary replace without corrupting the other scopes/attribute. Needs human review.

_m2_dt -> _stamp_document_genre (live_retriever.py:7261) — module-level function patched by STRING name via monkeypatch.setattr(lr, '_m2_dt', ...) in tests/polaris_graph/test_wave2_fetch_throughput.py (3 sites). Dynamic reference => NEEDS-ALIAS.

_w5_tier_batch_idx -> _tier_batch_index (live_retriever.py:7774) — used as a STRING DICT KEY that flows through data structures ('_row["_w5_tier_batch_idx"]', '_ev_row.pop("_w5_tier_batch_idx")') and is source-checked in scripts/iwire001_w5w2_unboundlocal_integration_fire_test.py ('_w5_tier_batch_idx' in seed_rows[0]). String data-contract => NEEDS-ALIAS. (Its sibling _w5_loop_idx, the value assigned, WAS renamed.)

rhsr_patched -> honest_sweep_module (harness_render_boundary_screen.py:57) — exists ONLY as a STRING literal (synthetic module name passed to importlib.util.spec_from_file_location and registered in sys.modules['rhsr_patched']), never as an identifier. Rule forbids editing string literals for SAFE-static rows. NEEDS-ALIAS/TEXT-ONLY reclass.

rhsr3_canary -> sweep_module_canary (iarch007_behavioral_canary.py:157) — exists ONLY as a STRING literal (module name arg to importlib.util.spec_from_file_location), not an identifier. Same reason as rhsr_patched.
rename.collection_after: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (unchanged from the green baseline; all 76 modified .py files py_compile cleanly). The 11 collection errors are the same pre-existing environment/dependency failures present before this work. Separately verified: the 3 runtime failures in tests/polaris_graph/blockers/test_g2_runsweep_blockers.py (test_iarch011_prb_*) FAIL identically on the clean stashed baseline, so they are pre-existing and NOT caused by any rename.

validate.notes:
All three gates PASS. The renames in /home/polaris/wt/phase2 (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2) are score-safe.

GATE 1 — Full test COLLECTION: PASS (matches baseline floor exactly).
  Result: "16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 11.44s"
  Baseline: collected=16738, errors=11. Both match. No regression from the rename work; the 11 errors are the known pre-existing environment/dependency collection failures.

GATE 2 — Config characterization: PASS.
  Command: pytest tests/test_config_registry.py tests/test_settings_models.py -q
  Result: "9 passed in 0.11s" — still green.

GATE 3 — ORACLE replay on covered outline path: PASS.
  Command: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=3 ... tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py --replay
  Artifact SHA-256: 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 — EQUALS the golden. Replay artifact is BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden (3184 bytes).
  Both controls pass: negative control valid (valid_negative_control=true) and positive/full loop valid (full_loop_ran=true, finish_outline ACCEPTED at turn 1). Final line: "ACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid".

The oracle files did NOT need the same rename — the covered outline path replayed byte-identical, so the oracle still references consistent symbols and behavior did not move. No oracle-path rename inconsistency observed.

codex_points: Reasonable to commit as a low-risk rename batch, but 'reference-complete' should be read as repository-STATIC completeness, not proof against every runtime/external reference.

pytest --collect-only is useful but incomplete: it imports test + application modules (catching broken module-level imports, decorators, base classes, import-time lookups) but does NOT execute test bodies, fixtures, most function bodies, lazy imports, env-gated branches, subprocess entry points, or optional integrations. An imported module can still hold a stale name inside an unexecuted function without collection noticing. py_compile validates syntax, not name resolution.
Baseline comparison would be STRONGER if the collected node IDs and the identities of the 11 errors match, rather than merely their counts (recommended hardening, not a blocker).

For repo-wide whole-token replacement, ordinary Python references should be complete: a static identifier cannot hide from a correctly-scoped token search. Residual risk is confined to references OUTSIDE that model: constructed strings ('old_'+'name'); reflection via getattr/globals/registries/import hooks/config files; contracts derived from __name__ or qualified names (CLI commands, routes, plugin registrations, metrics, schemas, pickled/serialized objects); generated files, non-Python assets, ignored files, downstream consumers outside the monorepo; runtime-only branches not exercised by selected tests; docstrings consumed by doctest/source snapshots/runtime introspection; accidental over-renaming of an unrelated same-spelling identifier in another scope.
The skip discipline directly addresses the most likely instances of these hazards (dynamic/string/source-grep references were all deferred).
Recommended completeness check: a final RAW SEARCH of every applied OLD token across all tracked file types, with every remaining occurrence classified — this is more direct than collection alone.

Byte-identical oracle replay is STRONG evidence for the covered outline path; valid controls confirm the equality came from a genuine completed run, not a shortcut/invalid replay. It shows no observable artifact change along that execution path and its transitive deps, but does NOT establish equivalence for other commands, lazy branches, integrations, or public-API consumers (narrow to one path).

Overall: evidence supports committing — known dynamic/ambiguous cases excluded, static refs mechanically updated, collection did not regress, characterization green, relevant oracle path identical. Residual risk is nonzero but confined to undiscovered dynamic/name-derived/external/unexecuted contracts, NOT ordinary in-repo static references.
Codex final token: RENAMES-SAFE. Tokens used: 8094.

summary: Execute the low-risk Plan V4 renames (SAFE-static symbols + TEXT-ONLY), gated by test-collection + oracle SHA + codex
